title: Mattermost Integration
summary: Mattermost integration via REST API v4 and WebSocket, covering bot account creation, team/channel configuration, thread vs flat reply modes, mention behavior, per-channel system prompt overrides, and self-hosted Slack alternative positioning.
key_concepts: Mattermost REST API v4 for sending messages
Bot account with personal access token
Thread replies vs flat channel posts

env_vars.name: MATTERMOST_TOKEN
env_vars.description: Personal access token for the bot account

env_vars.name: MATTERMOST_TEAM
env_vars.description: Team name to restrict bot to; if unset bot works across all teams

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway including Mattermost WebSocket listener

config_keys.description: Team name to restrict bot to
Channel names or IDs the bot responds in
Only respond when @mentioned (default: true for channels, false for DMs)
Reply in thread (default: true); false = post to channel root
Map of channel name/ID to system prompt override

code_examples.title: Create bot account
code_examples.code:
Mattermost > Main Menu > Integrations > Bot Accounts > Add Bot Account
Username: hermes-bot, Role: Member
Copy the personal access token shown after creation

code_examples.title: Mattermost config
code_examples.code:
mattermost:
  url: "https://mattermost.example.com"
  token: "${MATTERMOST_TOKEN}"
  team: "my-team"
  allowed_channels:
    - town-square
    - hermes-ai
  mention_only: true
  thread_reply: true

code_examples.title: Per-channel prompt override
code_examples.code:
mattermost:
  per_channel_prompts:
    hermes-ai: "You are a technical assistant."
    support: "You are a customer support agent."

gotchas: Bot accounts require System Admin to enable "Enable Bot Account Creation" in System Console > Integrations > Bot Accounts
Personal access tokens must be enabled by System Admin in System Console > Integrations > Integration Management
WebSocket connection uses /api/v4/websocket; firewall rules must allow WebSocket upgrades on the Mattermost port
thread_reply: false posts to the channel root which can be noisy in busy channels
Mention format in Mattermost is @username not @display_name
Rate limits apply per-user: Mattermost default is 300 requests/minute per user
full_text:
## Mattermost

Connect Hermes to [Mattermost](https://mattermost.com/) via REST API v4 and WebSocket.

### Setup

#### 1. Enable bot accounts and personal access tokens

In **System Console > Integrations**:
- Enable **Bot Account Creation**
- Enable **Personal Access Tokens** under Integration Management

#### 2. Create a bot account

Go to **Main Menu > Integrations > Bot Accounts > Add Bot Account**:
- Username: `hermes-bot`, Role: Member
- Copy the personal access token.

#### 3. Config

```yaml
mattermost:
  url: "https://mattermost.example.com"
  token: "${MATTERMOST_TOKEN}"
  team: "my-team"
  allowed_channels:
    - town-square
    - hermes-ai
  mention_only: true
  thread_reply: true
```

### Session model

Each Mattermost user gets their own isolated Hermes session. DMs and channel conversations are separate sessions.

### Thread vs flat replies

By default (`thread_reply: true`), Hermes replies inside a thread. Set `thread_reply: false` to post to the channel root.

### Mention behavior

In channels, Hermes responds only when @mentioned. In DMs it always responds.

### Per-channel prompts

```yaml
mattermost:
  per_channel_prompts:
    hermes-ai: "You are a technical assistant."
    support: "You are a customer support agent."
```

### Direct messages

The bot accepts DMs from any team member. Use `allowed_channels` with DM channel IDs to restrict further.
